summary: Cross-validated code_packages escalation: module-rung correctness must move to P0 if Phase 002 keeps promising a module rung, but the current fq_name-prefix-populated table is not sufficient; file_path-derived ownership is the correct P0 fallback.

title: fq_name is lexical/symbol-qualified, not package-qualified
recommendation: Use fqName only for symbol/class display rungs; derive file/module ownership from CodeNode.filePath.

title: Iteration 002 escalates module-rung resolution to P0, but not the current table design verbatim
recommendation: Amend Phase 002 P0 to include deterministic module resolution from file_path; keep code_packages P1 only as an optimization over that policy.

title: Live DB fq_name samples fail dot-splitting for module recovery
evidence: live sqlite: code_nodes samples APIClient.get, AdvisorHookMetricsCollector.record, module fq_name=index/providers/eval, export fq_name=*
recommendation: Add trace tests proving class methods, top-level functions, module nodes, and star re-exports do not derive module from fqName prefixes.

title: Class/method and parameter nesting make dots lexical separators
recommendation: Do not parse module names from dotted fqName segments; document fqName as lexical identity.

title: Re-exports and anonymous/default exports block fq_name-based package inference
recommendation: Represent barrel re-export provenance explicitly or use exporting file_path as the trace module; do not infer source module from fqName.

title: Performance alone does not justify P0 schema migration at current scale
recommendation: Benchmark before adding a package-table join for speed; correctness is the schema escalation trigger.
